boundary: This manifest prepares cloud execution only. It does not run cloud, authorize release, or authorize new public RTX speedup wording.

diagnostic_validation_reruns.allowed_claim_today: bounded prepared facility service-coverage decision sub-path may enter claim review; no KNN assignment or ranking speedup claim

baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

diagnostic_validation_reruns.benchmark_contract: coverage_threshold_prepared decision mode has an RTX phase artifact and same-scale CPU oracle parity for the bounded service-coverage decision
diagnostic_validation_reruns.claim_scope: prepared OptiX fixed-radius threshold traversal for facility service-coverage decisions
diagnostic_validation_reruns.non_claim: not a ranked nearest-depot, KNN fallback-assignment, or facility-location optimizer claim
diagnostic_validation_reruns.optix_performance_note: Explicit coverage_threshold_prepared mode uses prepared OptiX fixed-radius threshold traversal for service-coverage decisions only; ranked nearest-depot assignment remains outside the OptiX claim.
preconditions: Use the prepared coverage-threshold profiler, not KNN ranking output.
Interpret with the Goal887 RTX artifact and Goal920 same-scale CPU oracle baseline.
Do not start a pod only for this app; rerun only in a consolidated regression batch.
diagnostic_validation_reruns.readiness_blocker: ranked nearest-depot assignment remains outside the OptiX claim; only the service-coverage decision sub-path is traversal-backed

diagnostic_validation_reruns.allowed_claim_today: prepared ray/triangle any-hit scalar pose-count sub-path may enter claim review; no full robot-planning speedup claim

baseline_review_contract.claim_limit: scalar pose-count collision screening only; not full robot planning, kinematics, CCD, or witness-row output
baseline_review_contract.comparable_metric_scope: prepared scalar colliding-pose count for the same poses, edges, obstacles, and iteration policy
baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

diagnostic_validation_reruns.benchmark_contract: RTX timing must split prepared-scene build/reuse, ray buffer packing, OptiX traversal, compact output, and oracle validation
diagnostic_validation_reruns.claim_scope: prepared OptiX ray/triangle any-hit pose-flag summary
diagnostic_validation_reruns.non_claim: not continuous collision detection, full robot kinematics, or mesh-engine replacement
diagnostic_validation_reruns.optix_performance_note: Uses OptiX ray/triangle any-hit traversal and is the best current OptiX flagship candidate; compact row-output modes avoid returning full witness rows, while prepared scalar hit-count and prepared native pose-flag summary modes report native continuation and avoid per-ray row materialization. Edge witnesses still require row mode.
preconditions: OptiX ray/triangle any-hit prepared symbols must be exported.
Use the phase profiler rather than raw app CLI timing for final claim review.
Cloud performance timing must use --skip-validation after Goal763 focused correctness passes.
Use packed_arrays input mode to avoid per-ray Python object construction in large cloud timing.
Use pose_count result mode for scalar native summary timing after pose-flag correctness is established.
diagnostic_validation_reruns.readiness_blocker: RTX 4090 evidence covers prepared scalar pose-count traversal only; full robot kinematics and witness-row output remain outside the claim

global_preconditions: RTX-class NVIDIA GPU with RT cores; do not use GTX 1070-class hardware for claims.
OptiX backend built from the checked-out commit before timing.
RTDL_SOURCE_COMMIT exported and non-empty before running any command.
Bootstrap check passes before app commands.
Diagnostic rerun rows must not include --skip-validation.
policy: Do not start or stop a cloud pod per app. Run this as one batched session, copy artifacts after each row or small group, and stop the pod before local review.
purpose: One-pod post-Goal1048 RTX command manifest. It prioritizes validation-enabled reruns for diagnostic-only facility and robot paths, then packages remaining not-reviewed rows for same-semantics review evidence.

same_semantics_review_candidates.allowed_claim_today: prepared DB compact-summary traversal/filter/grouping sub-path may enter claim review; no DBMS or SQL-engine speedup claim

baseline_review_contract.claim_limit: prepared DB sub-path only; not a DBMS or SQL-engine speedup claim
baseline_review_contract.comparable_metric_scope: compact_summary prepared DB query result for the same scenario/copies/iterations
baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

same_semantics_review_candidates.benchmark_contract: Goal941 provides real RTX compact-summary artifacts with native DB phase totals, strict output mode, and no row materialization operations for sales_risk and regional_dashboard
same_semantics_review_candidates.claim_scope: prepared OptiX DB session behavior and Python/interface cost split
same_semantics_review_candidates.non_claim: not a SQL engine claim and not a broad RTX RT-core app speedup claim
same_semantics_review_candidates.optix_performance_note: Uses real OptiX DB BVH candidate discovery and native C++ exact filtering/grouping. App-level performance is still limited by Python/ctypes preparation, candidate bitset copy-back, grouped-row decoding, and row materialization unless materialization-free compact_summary is selected; native-continuation metadata is active only for compact DB summaries that avoid row/group materialization.
preconditions: OptiX DB backend must be available.
Use prepared-session profiler, not only one-shot CLI timing.
same_semantics_review_candidates.readiness_blocker: DB claims must stay limited to compact-summary prepared sub-paths; no SQL engine, DBMS, full dashboard, row-materializing, or broad whole-app speedup claim is allowed

baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

same_semantics_review_candidates.claim_scope: prepared OptiX DB session behavior and Python/interface cost split
same_semantics_review_candidates.non_claim: not a SQL engine claim and not a broad RTX RT-core app speedup claim
same_semantics_review_candidates.optix_performance_note: Uses real OptiX DB BVH candidate discovery and native C++ exact filtering/grouping. App-level performance is still limited by Python/ctypes preparation, candidate bitset copy-back, grouped-row decoding, and row materialization unless materialization-free compact_summary is selected; native-continuation metadata is active only for compact DB summaries that avoid row/group materialization.
preconditions: OptiX DB backend must be available.
Use prepared-session profiler, not only one-shot CLI timing.
same_semantics_review_candidates.readiness_blocker: DB claims must stay limited to compact-summary prepared sub-paths; no SQL engine, DBMS, full dashboard, row-materializing, or broad whole-app speedup claim is allowed

same_semantics_review_candidates.activation_gate: Regression artifact must pass strict mode on RTX hardware for visibility, native BFS graph-ray, and native triangle graph-ray; higher-level graph-system exclusions must remain explicit and independent review must accept the artifact.

baseline_review_contract.claim_limit: bounded graph RT sub-paths only: visibility any-hit plus BFS/triangle candidate generation; not shortest-path, graph database, distributed graph analytics, or whole-app graph-system acceleration
baseline_review_contract.comparable_metric_scope: strict graph visibility-edge, native BFS graph-ray, and native triangle-count graph-ray row-digest results for the same copies semantics
baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

same_semantics_review_candidates.claim_scope: OptiX prepared ray/triangle any-hit count for graph visibility-edge summary filtering plus native OptiX graph-ray traversal candidate generation for BFS and triangle-count
same_semantics_review_candidates.non_claim: not shortest-path, graph database, distributed graph analytics, or whole-app graph-system acceleration; BFS visited/frontier bookkeeping and triangle set-intersection remain outside RT traversal
same_semantics_review_candidates.optix_performance_note: Explicit visibility_edges mode maps candidate graph edges to ray/triangle any-hit traversal; summary mode uses prepared any-hit count to avoid row materialization. Embree BFS/triangle-count are ray-traversal candidate-generation paths; OptiX BFS and triangle_count now have an explicit native graph-ray mode behind RTDL_OPTIX_GRAPH_MODE=native, and graph summary mode uses native C++ continuation after rows are produced. The default remains host-indexed until RTX validation.
same_semantics_review_candidates.reason_deferred: Goal929 passed this bounded graph gate on RTX 3090. Keep the command available for consolidated regression reruns: visibility maps candidate graph edges to a prepared OptiX any-hit count summary, while BFS and triangle-count use explicit native OptiX graph-ray mode for candidate generation.

same_semantics_review_candidates.allowed_claim_today: bounded prepared count-summary path may enter claim review; no whole-app hotspot-screening speedup claim

baseline_review_contract.claim_limit: prepared compact summary only; not nearest-row or whole-app speedup
baseline_review_contract.comparable_metric_scope: prepared compact fixed-radius summary for the same generated households/events/facilities and radius
baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

same_semantics_review_candidates.benchmark_contract: prepared count-summary mode has a reviewed RTX phase artifact plus same-scale Embree baseline parity; public claims still require final claim-review packaging
same_semantics_review_candidates.claim_scope: prepared OptiX fixed-radius count traversal for hotspot summaries
same_semantics_review_candidates.non_claim: not a whole-app hotspot-screening speedup claim and not a neighbor-row output claim
same_semantics_review_candidates.optix_performance_note: Explicit count_summary_prepared mode uses prepared OptiX fixed-radius count traversal and compact hotspot summaries; rows mode is not the RT-core claim path.
preconditions: Use the prepared count-summary profiler, not neighbor-row output.
Interpret with the Goal917 RTX artifact and Goal919 same-scale Embree baseline review.
Do not start a pod only for this app; rerun only in a consolidated regression batch.
same_semantics_review_candidates.readiness_blocker: Goal917 and Goal919 cover the bounded prepared count-summary path only; neighbor-row output and whole-app hotspot analytics remain outside the claim

same_semantics_review_candidates.activation_gate: Re-enter claim review only after native-kernel tuning shows competitive timing while the default host-indexed path remains clearly separated.

baseline_review_contract.claim_limit: experimental prepared road-hazard summary gate only; not default public app behavior or full GIS/routing speedup
baseline_review_contract.comparable_metric_scope: prepared road-hazard native OptiX summary result on the same copies and priority-threshold semantics
baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

same_semantics_review_candidates.claim_scope: native OptiX segment/polygon traversal for compact road-hazard summaries
same_semantics_review_candidates.non_claim: not default public road-hazard behavior and not a full GIS/routing speedup claim
same_semantics_review_candidates.optix_performance_note: Default segment/polygon OptiX app path remains conservative, but the prepared road-hazard summary profiler reuses a polygon BVH and runs native OptiX custom-AABB segment/polygon traversal for compact hazard summaries.
same_semantics_review_candidates.reason_deferred: Goal929 proved strict native road-hazard parity on RTX 3090, but native timing was slower than CPU. Goal933 now measures a prepared polygon-BVH path so warm query time is separated from setup.

same_semantics_review_candidates.activation_gate: Regression artifacts must keep phases separated and top-level claims limited to candidate discovery.

baseline_review_contract.claim_limit: native-assisted candidate-discovery plus native exact continuation path only; no full app RTX speedup claim without same-semantics review
baseline_review_contract.comparable_metric_scope: native-assisted OptiX LSI/PIP candidate-discovery phase plus native C++ exact area continuation
baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

same_semantics_review_candidates.claim_scope: OptiX native-assisted LSI/PIP candidate discovery for bounded polygon-pair overlap
same_semantics_review_candidates.non_claim: not a monolithic GPU polygon-area kernel and not a full app RTX speedup claim
same_semantics_review_candidates.optix_performance_note: Public app exposes OptiX native-assisted LSI/PIP positive candidate discovery, then native C++ exact grid-cell area continuation. This is real traversal-assisted filtering plus native continuation, not a monolithic GPU polygon-area kernel.
same_semantics_review_candidates.reason_deferred: Goal929 proved phase-separated RTX candidate-discovery timing, exact continuation timing, and parity for this native-assisted polygon overlap sub-path. Keep this command for consolidated regression reruns.

same_semantics_review_candidates.activation_gate: Regression artifacts must keep chunk-copies=20 unless larger chunks are fixed, phases separated, and top-level claims limited to candidate discovery.

baseline_review_contract.claim_limit: native-assisted candidate-discovery plus native exact continuation path only; no full app RTX speedup claim without same-semantics review

baseline_review_contract.forbidden_comparison: Do not compare a scalar/prepared RTX sub-path against a whole-app, row-output, validation-included, or different-result-mode baseline.

This is real traversal-assisted filtering plus native continuation, not a monolithic GPU Jaccard kernel.

same_semantics_review_candidates.non_claim: not an exact Hausdorff distance, KNN-row, or nearest-neighbor ranking speedup claim
same_semantics_review_candidates.optix_performance_note: Default exact-distance mode uses KNN rows through CUDA-style kernels; explicit directed_threshold_prepared mode uses prepared OptiX fixed-radius threshold traversal for Hausdorff <= radius decisions only.

same_semantics_review_candidates.non_claim: not a Barnes-Hut opening-rule, force-vector reduction, or N-body solver speedup claim
